title: Consumer Behavior
summary: Understand how and why consumers make purchase decisions, and the factors that influence buying behavior.
content_html:
Consumer behavior studies how individuals select, purchase, and use products to satisfy needs and desires.

Maslow's Hierarchy of Needs

People are motivated by needs in a hierarchy (bottom = most basic):

  1. Physiological (food, water, shelter)
  2. Safety (security, stability)
  3. Social/Love (relationships, belonging)
  4. Esteem (status, recognition)
  5. Self-actualization (personal growth)

The Buyer Decision Process

  1. Problem recognition: consumer identifies a need
  2. Information search: research options
  3. Evaluation of alternatives: compare options
  4. Purchase decision: buy
  5. Post-purchase behavior: satisfaction or regret

Factors Influencing Behavior

  • Cultural: culture, subculture, social class
  • Social: reference groups, family, roles
  • Personal: age, occupation, lifestyle, personality
  • Psychological: motivation, perception, learning

Cognitive Dissonance

Post-purchase discomfort when the chosen product underperforms expectations. Marketers reduce it through reassurance and strong after-sale support.

exercises.title: Cognitive dissonance
exercises.prompt: Cognitive dissonance after purchase refers to:
options: Post-purchase doubt or regret about the buying decision
exercises.answer: Post-purchase doubt or regret about the buying decision

exercises.title: Social factor
exercises.prompt: Which is an example of a SOCIAL factor influencing consumer behavior?

exercises.title: Self-actualization
exercises.prompt: Self-actualization (top of Maslow's hierarchy) refers to:
options: Personal growth and fulfilling one's potential
exercises.answer: Personal growth and fulfilling one's potential

exercises.prompt: Perception and motivation are examples of which type of factor?

exercises.title: Post-purchase
exercises.prompt: Post-purchase behavior in the buyer process includes:
options: Satisfaction, loyalty, or regret after buying
exercises.answer: Satisfaction, loyalty, or regret after buying

exercises.title: Steps count
